actionable_feedback: Correct the incorrect slack value quoted for O2 in the memo. Replace '5900 slack' with the correct slack: 130,800 - 104,900 = 25,900, and update any dependent summary lines/tables.

Expose workbook formulas (not values-only cells) so the deterministic precheck can validate formula visibility. Ensure cells contain formulas referencing source inputs rather than only cached numbers.
Add a Validation Checklist sheet in the workbook listing each derived value with explicit sheet and cell addresses (e.g., 'Baseline metal impact R1 = Sheet:Baseline!C5 (formula) -> 273').

Clarify ambiguity in option wording: explicitly state whether 'by -12%' and 'by -18%' are interpreted as relative multipliers or absolute percentage-point shifts. If both were analyzed, mark the primary interpretation and show both computations with cell-level citations.

Re-run the artifact build with a check that openpyxl (or an equivalent parser) will be able to read the workbook in the evaluation environment, or include a formula-export (CSV or sheet) that lists cell formulas to enable programmatic verification even if openpyxl is unavailable.

Cross-check all memo summary numbers (slack, totals, deltas) against the workbook cells and add a short 'Cross-check' section in the memo listing the specific cell references used to compute each summary number.

Fixing the arithmetic and ensuring formula visibility and explicit cell citations will raise confidence to full credit.
